mapping.a_element: fission neutron chain reaction (each fission liberates neutrons that induce further fissions)
mapping.b_element: SIR transmission process (each infected individual infects further susceptibles)

mapping.a_element: neutron multiplication factor / criticality (critical mass, k around 1)
mapping.b_element: epidemic reproduction / critical boosting threshold (R0-type threshold above which the outbreak grows)

mapping.a_element: self-termination of the reaction (fissile burnup / hydrodynamic disassembly halting multiplication)
mapping.b_element: depletion of the susceptible pool that halts the outbreak (and the 'silent exposures' that dry up once suppression succeeds)

mapping.a_element: total explosive yield (integrated energy released by the chain reaction)
mapping.b_element: total attack rate / final epidemic size (integrated fraction infected)

mapping.a_element: closed-form Bethe-Feynman / Compton yield formula for the integrated output
mapping.b_element: closed-form final-size relation linking total attack rate to the driving parameters

brief_justification: Both papers derive a closed-form expression for the total integrated output of a supercritical branching (multiplicative) process governed by a critical threshold and self-terminated by exhaustion of its driver: paper A's fission chain reaction with its criticality/critical-mass threshold and Bethe-Feynman yield formula plays the same role as paper B's SIR transmission with its reproduction/boosting threshold and closed-form final-size (attack-rate) relation, so the yield formula and the epidemic final-size relation are the same machinery across fields.
